prompt: In SAM, what does an entitlement record primarily represent?
choices: A discovered installation awaiting normalization
A right to use software, typically derived from purchases, agreements, or subscriptions
A workflow task for license reclamation only
A one-time approval to install software on a single device
explanation: Entitlements represent use rights and are the foundation for comparing license position against installed and consumed software.

prompt: A true-up is approaching and the publisher questions available license rights. Which entitlement attribute is most critical to verify first?
choices: UI policy names on the entitlement form
License metric and quantity aligned to contract terms
Short description color settings
Number of attachments on unrelated software models
explanation: License metric and quantity define the legal measurement basis and total rights, which directly drive compliance calculations.

prompt: What is a common reason to split rights into multiple entitlement records for the same software model?
choices: To increase discovery scan frequency
To separate rights by metric, term, or restriction so reconciliation applies the correct rules
To avoid using contracts in SAM
To convert named user licenses into hardware assets
explanation: Different terms and metrics require separate entitlement records to preserve accurate compliance logic and audit traceability.

prompt: Which action best improves confidence that entitlements are audit-ready?
choices: Store purchase proof and contractual evidence linked to entitlement records
Delete expired entitlements immediately without history
Move entitlement quantities into custom text fields
Disable reconciliation jobs during quarter close
explanation: Attaching clear entitlement evidence supports defensible license positions and reduces audit response time.

prompt: When an organization moves to subscription licensing, what SAM update is most important?
choices: Disable publisher data management
Map subscriptions to the correct entitlement structures and terms so renewals and consumption are tracked accurately
Treat all subscriptions as perpetual rights
Exclude subscription installs from reconciliation
explanation: Subscription licensing introduces term-based rights, so entitlement structure and renewal tracking must reflect subscription conditions.

prompt: What is the purpose of the Strategic Execution Dashboard in SPM?
choices: To monitor strategic objective progress and execution health in one place for portfolio decisions
To replace all project workbench functionality
To manage discovery patterns for CMDB
To run monthly software reconciliation jobs
explanation: The dashboard consolidates objective, investment, and delivery indicators so leadership can evaluate strategy execution effectively.

prompt: A CIO asks why a strategic objective is off track. Which dashboard capability should a portfolio analyst use first?
choices: Delete low-performing investments from the plan
Drill from objective scorecards into contributing initiatives and their status indicators
Export all data to spreadsheets and stop using SPM
Hide cost and resource metrics for simplicity
explanation: Drill-down analysis links objective performance to underlying work items, helping identify where execution risks are occurring.

prompt: Which KPI combination is most useful on a strategic execution dashboard?
choices: Only number of open incidents
Only total active users in the platform
Objective progress, investment health, and milestone delivery trends
Hardware lifecycle end dates
explanation: Combining strategic progress and execution metrics creates a balanced view for portfolio steering conversations.

prompt: How does dashboard filtering improve governance meetings in SPM?
choices: It removes the need for strategic objectives
It ensures all initiatives are always marked green
It lets leaders focus by portfolio, business unit, or timeframe to prioritize corrective action
It automatically approves all investment changes
explanation: Targeted filtering helps decision-makers isolate issues quickly and act on the highest-priority strategy execution gaps.

prompt: If objective progress looks healthy but delivery dates keep slipping, what is the best interpretation?
choices: Dashboard data is always wrong when dates slip
The strategic plan should be deleted
There may be reporting lag or weak milestone discipline, requiring deeper execution review
Financial planning is no longer required
explanation: Divergence between strategic score and delivery timelines is a signal to inspect milestone tracking quality and execution dependencies.
